project.name: Researcher
project.description: Personal Telegram bot that accepts free-form research requests with files/links, processes them through Google NotebookLM via notebooklm-py, and returns generated research outputs. Runs as an OpenClaw agent on Mac mini.
project.mvp_goal: Turn Telegram into a personal interface for running research workflows through Google NotebookLM.

dependencies.name: notebooklm-py
dependencies.role: Primary adapter for Google NotebookLM integration

dependencies.name: Supabase
dependencies.role: Metadata storage, project registry, search index

dependencies.name: OpenClaw
dependencies.role: Agent runtime and Telegram routing layer

architecture.flow: Telegram -> OpenClaw -> Researcher Agent -> notebooklm-py / NotebookLM -> Supabase -> Telegram
architecture.deployment_phase_1: Local on Mac mini with polling
architecture.model: Separate repository, later plugs into OpenClaw as standalone agent

user_stories.title: Create a new research task
user_stories.description: User sends a text request and materials in Telegram, bot creates a research project.
acceptance_criteria: User can send a free-form text request
User can attach one or more materials
Bot creates a new research project
Messages within 2-minute window are grouped into one project
Bot sends confirmation of task acceptance

user_stories.title: Support mixed input types
user_stories.description: User sends various material types through a single Telegram interface.
acceptance_criteria: Bot accepts plain links
Bot accepts YouTube URLs
Bot accepts PDF files
Bot accepts audio files
Bot accepts images
Bot accepts video files
Bot accepts other files when format is supported by NotebookLM/notebooklm-py

user_stories.title: Execute research through NotebookLM
user_stories.description: Bot uses NotebookLM as the main engine for generating research outputs.
acceptance_criteria: Bot uses notebooklm-py as integration layer
Materials are uploaded to NotebookLM
Requested output type is triggered within NotebookLM capabilities
Final result is returned to Telegram

user_stories.title: Free-form intent (no menu)
user_stories.description: User writes requests in natural language without menus or fixed commands.
acceptance_criteria: User can write requests naturally (e.g. 'make an audio review', 'prepare a summary')
Bot interprets requests within NotebookLM-supported capabilities
No menu-first UX required

user_stories.title: Progress status updates
user_stories.description: Bot shows processing progress in Telegram.
acceptance_criteria: Status: task accepted
Status: materials uploaded / preparing
Status: sent to NotebookLM
Status: result generating
Status: result ready

user_stories.title: Return results to Telegram
user_stories.description: Bot delivers research results back to the same Telegram chat.
acceptance_criteria: Result is sent to the same chat
Bot can send single message
Bot can send multiple messages if format requires it
Bot can attach files (audio, images, documents) when needed

user_stories.title: Context memory (24 hours)
user_stories.description: Bot remembers current task context for follow-up requests.
acceptance_criteria: Bot remembers current task context for 24 hours
User can send follow-ups like 'make it shorter', 'translate it', 'now make audio version'
After 24 hours, task is no longer active context by default

user_stories.title: Parallel projects
user_stories.description: User can run multiple research tasks simultaneously.
acceptance_criteria: User can have multiple active projects at once
Each project maps to a separate NotebookLM notebook/project
Bot tracks status of each project independently

user_stories.title: Persist project metadata in Supabase
user_stories.description: All research projects and metadata are stored in Supabase.
acceptance_criteria: Project stores: project_id, user_id, project_name, original_user_request, status, notebooklm_project_id, result_type, created_at, updated_at
Result metadata also stored: telegram_message_refs, result_summary, notebooklm_result_ref

user_stories.title: Auto-name projects with rename
user_stories.description: Projects are named automatically but can be renamed later.
acceptance_criteria: Project name is auto-generated from user request
User can rename the project later
Updated name is saved in Supabase and used in search

user_stories.title: Search past projects
user_stories.description: User can find old research projects.
acceptance_criteria: Search by project name
Search by original user request text
Search by attached links/files
Search by status
Search by date

user_stories.title: List all projects
user_stories.description: User can see a list of all research projects.
acceptance_criteria: Bot shows project list with: name, creation date, status, material count, request snippet

user_stories.title: Add material to existing project
user_stories.description: User can add new material to an already existing project.
acceptance_criteria: User can write 'add this file to project X'
Bot resolves target project
Material is attached to the project
Project updated in both Supabase and NotebookLM

user_stories.title: View project contents
user_stories.description: User can see which materials belong to a project.
acceptance_criteria: Bot shows material list for the project
Each material shows: type/name, date added, processing status
Material statuses: received, uploading, added_to_notebooklm, error, used_in_result

user_stories.title: Handle unsupported formats gracefully
user_stories.description: Bot does not fail entirely because of one unsupported file.
acceptance_criteria: Bot attempts automatic conversion for unsupported formats
If conversion fails, bot notifies user about specific file
Remaining materials continue processing
Failed file gets error status

user_stories.title: Retry on generation errors
user_stories.description: Bot is resilient to temporary integration failures.
acceptance_criteria: Bot automatically retries on temporary NotebookLM failures
If retries fail, bot shows final error reason
Error is tracked at project level

user_stories.title: Cancel and delete projects
acceptance_criteria: User can cancel active task
User can delete old project
Deletion removes from both Supabase and NotebookLM
Both actions require confirmation via inline buttons (Confirm / Cancel)

user_stories.title: File size limit handling
user_stories.description: Clear error messages when files exceed size limits.
acceptance_criteria: Bot relies on Telegram and NotebookLM native limits
When limit exceeded, bot explains error clearly

research_materials.material_type: text (link, youtube, pdf, audio, image, video, file)
research_materials.source_value: text (URL, file path, or Telegram file_id)

error_handling.unsupported_format: try auto-convert, then notify user and continue with remaining materials
error_handling.generation_failure: automatic retry with exponential backoff, then report final error
error_handling.file_size_exceeded: rely on Telegram/NotebookLM limits, explain error clearly

phases.name: Core Ingestion
tasks: Set up Telegram bot with polling
phases.verification: User sends message + file -> project created in Supabase -> user sees task accepted status

phases.name: NotebookLM Integration
tasks: Trigger result generation based on user request
Return results to Telegram (text, files, multiple messages)
Implement retry logic with exponential backoff
Show all 5 status stages during processing
phases.verification: User sends 'make audio review' + PDF -> bot processes via NotebookLM -> result returned in Telegram

phases.name: Persistence and Search
tasks: Persist all project metadata and material statuses
phases.verification: User searches for past project -> finds it. User lists projects -> sees all with metadata.

phases.name: Project Lifecycle
tasks: Add material to existing project by name
Cancel active task with button confirmation
Delete project from Supabase + NotebookLM with button confirmation
Handle file size limit errors with clear messages
phases.verification: User adds file to existing project. User deletes project -> removed from both systems. Follow-up within 24h works.

out_of_scope: Full artifact history within a project
open_questions: What is the actual feature coverage of notebooklm-py?
Are there NotebookLM rate limits for automation?
How does the agent plug into OpenClaw routing?
How does notebooklm-py authenticate with Google/NotebookLM?
How to map free-form requests to NotebookLM generation actions?
Same Supabase instance as MaxAiDeveloper or new project?
